diagnosis.attention: Both runs pass native architecture and reload audits. Gate 6N attention has higher normalized entropy (~0.835-0.837 vs ~0.792-0.795) and lower maximum weight (~0.055 vs ~0.075-0.077), indicating a more diffuse/non-collapsed attention distribution. Its strongest listed association remains log_inverse_kz_relative (about -0.32), while q/source-fraction associations are weak (about -0.09 to -0.10).

final.interpretation: Gate 6N final improves both relative metrics, raw RMSE, shape, hotspot/top5/strong-q and background RMSE, but scale log-RMSE is worse; final is not a checkpoint-selection replacement.

diagnosis.limitations: This closeout is valid_iid-only and uses non-contemporaneous training commits (devbox 414ab43; WSL2 0c49084) with one evaluator commit 1cf5bff; no test, hard, or sealed-IID data were opened. Declared log files were absent on both remotes, but loss_summary and all checkpoint/prediction artifacts were present.

point_global_best.interpretation: Gate 6N improves point-global, raw CV-weighted, shape, hotspot and correlation, while scale log-RMSE worsens and amplitude shifts below 1.0; primary point-global remains above the 20% gate.

sample_first_best.interpretation: Gate 6N slightly improves sample-first relative RMSE and raw/shape metrics, but scale log-RMSE is worse; both sample-first values are below 20% (diagnostic only, not primary selection).
